message:
rename: pii-wasm → @teamchong/textsift

The "pii" prefix is wrong on two counts: the upstream model
(`openai/privacy-filter`) covers a broader privacy span set than
strict PII, and the package will gain a custom-rule engine that
expands beyond privacy entirely. The "wasm" suffix is wrong because
WebGPU and native are also first-class backends.

Mechanical rename across all sources, docs, and the docs-site:
  package name           pii-wasm                 → textsift
  scoped npm name        @yourorg/pii-wasm        → @teamchong/textsift
  GitHub repo URL        teamchong/pii-wasm       → teamchong/textsift
  inline TS file         pii-wasm-inline.ts       → textsift-inline.ts
  WASM artifacts         pii.wasm / pii-mt.wasm   → textsift.wasm /
                                                    textsift-mt.wasm
  exported identifiers   PII_WASM_*, PiiWasm*,    → TEXTSIFT_*,
                         loadPiiWasm*               Textsift*,
                                                    loadTextsift*
  zig --name             pii / pii-mt             → textsift /
                                                    textsift-mt

Public class `PrivacyFilter` is unchanged for now — model is still
privacy-filter and existing call sites stay valid. Class will be
revisited when the rule-engine API lands.

Smoke parity preserved (2/2 spans). Typecheck + build clean.

files.patch:
@@ -1,23 +1,23 @@
-# pii-wasm
+# textsift
 
 **PII detection and redaction for the browser and Node, powered by `openai/privacy-filter`.**
 
 Runs entirely on the user's device — WebGPU on capable browsers, WebAssembly + SIMD128 everywhere else. No backend, no network calls to inference services, no text ever leaves the device.
 
 > The only npm package that runs `openai/privacy-filter` client-side with both GPU and CPU paths.
 
-[**Docs**](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/) · [**Quickstart**](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/quickstart/) · [**API**](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/api/) · [**Benchmarks**](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/benchmarks/)
+[**Docs**](https://teamchong.github.io/textsift/) · [**Quickstart**](https://teamchong.github.io/textsift/quickstart/) · [**API**](https://teamchong.github.io/textsift/api/) · [**Benchmarks**](https://teamchong.github.io/textsift/benchmarks/)
 
 ## Install
 
 ```sh
-npm install pii-wasm
+npm install textsift
 ```
 
 ## Use
 
 ```ts
-import { PrivacyFilter } from "pii-wasm";
+import { PrivacyFilter } from "textsift";
 
 const filter = await PrivacyFilter.create();
 
@@ -39,19 +39,19 @@ Detection without applying redactions:
 const { spans, containsPii } = await filter.detect(text);
 ```
 
-Batch inputs, custom markers, per-category enabling — see the [API reference](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/api/).
+Batch inputs, custom markers, per-category enabling — see the [API reference](https://teamchong.github.io/textsift/api/).
 
 ## Why
 
 OpenAI released [`openai/privacy-filter`](https://huggingface.co/openai/privacy-filter) on 2026-04-20 — a 1.5B-parameter MoE (50M active) bidirectional token classifier for PII detection. Apache 2.0. State-of-the-art on PII-Masking-300k (96% F1).
 
-The official SDK is Python. `transformers.js` runs it on WebGPU but fails on CPU — ORT-Web's WASM bundle is missing `GatherBlockQuantized` and `MatMulNBits` kernels. **pii-wasm is the only package that runs this model end-to-end in the browser on both GPU and CPU**, with a single public API.
+The official SDK is Python. `transformers.js` runs it on WebGPU but fails on CPU — ORT-Web's WASM bundle is missing `GatherBlockQuantized` and `MatMulNBits` kernels. **textsift is the only package that runs this model end-to-end in the browser on both GPU and CPU**, with a single public API.
 
 ## Performance
 
-M-series MacBook / Chromium 147, median of 5 runs after 2 warmups. Full table in [/benchmarks](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/benchmarks/):
+M-series MacBook / Chromium 147, median of 5 runs after 2 warmups. Full table in [/benchmarks](https://teamchong.github.io/textsift/benchmarks/):
 
-| | tjs (WebGPU) | **pii-wasm (WebGPU)** | speedup |
+| | tjs (WebGPU) | **textsift (WebGPU)** | speedup |
 |---|---:|---:|---:|
 | Short input | 29 ms | **7 ms** | **4.1×** |
 | Medium input | 39 ms | **12 ms** | **3.2×** |
@@ -76,7 +76,7 @@ const wasm = await PrivacyFilter.create({ backend: "wasm" });    // universal
 const auto = await PrivacyFilter.create({ backend: "auto" });    // tjs baseline
 ```
 
-All three produce byte-identical spans on the same input. See [/backends](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/backends/).
+All three produce byte-identical spans on the same input. See [/backends](https://teamchong.github.io/textsift/backends/).
 
 ## Architecture
 
@@ -103,13 +103,13 @@ All three produce byte-identical spans on the same input. See [/backends](https:
       ↑ cached in OPFS ↑
 ```
 
-Full architecture details in [/architecture](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/architecture/).
+Full architecture details in [/architecture](https://teamchong.github.io/textsift/architecture/).
 
 ## Caveats
 
 `openai/privacy-filter` is a detection aid, **not an anonymization guarantee**. No dedicated SSN / passport label. English-first (Japanese ~88% F1, other languages untested). Short text under-contextualizes.
 
-See the [caveats page](https://teamchong.github.io/pii-wasm/caveats/) and OpenAI's [model card](https://cdn.openai.com/pdf/c66281ed-b638-456a-8ce1-97e9f5264a90/OpenAI-Privacy-Filter-Model-Card.pdf) before treating redacted output as compliance-safe.
+See the [caveats page](https://teamchong.github.io/textsift/caveats/) and OpenAI's [model card](https://cdn.openai.com/pdf/c66281ed-b638-456a-8ce1-97e9f5264a90/OpenAI-Privacy-Filter-Model-Card.pdf) before treating redacted output as compliance-safe.
 
 ## Development

@@ -3,7 +3,7 @@ - pii-wasm demo + textsift demo -

pii-wasm

+

textsift

Browser-native PII detection via openai/privacy-filter. Everything runs locally — no network calls to an inference service.

   "type": "module",
   "scripts": {

@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
 ---
 title: API Reference
-description: Full surface of the pii-wasm public API.
+description: Full surface of the textsift public API.
 ---
 
-`pii-wasm` has a narrow public surface: one class and a handful of types. Everything else (tokenization, chunking, Viterbi CRF, BIOES merging, backend selection, OPFS caching) is internal.
+`textsift` has a narrow public surface: one class and a handful of types. Everything else (tokenization, chunking, Viterbi CRF, BIOES merging, backend selection, OPFS caching) is internal.
 
 ## `PrivacyFilter.create(options?)`

@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 ---
 title: Architecture
-description: How pii-wasm turns text into redacted output, from tokenizer to spans.
+description: How textsift turns text into redacted output, from tokenizer to spans.
 ---
 
 ## Pipeline
@@ -79,7 +79,7 @@ Thin wrapper around `AutoModelForTokenClassification`. We bypass `pipeline('toke
 
 ### Zig + WASM (Stage 1)
 
-Hand-written kernels in `src/zig/wasm_exports.zig`, compiled with `-target wasm32-freestanding -mcpu=generic+simd128+relaxed_simd -O ReleaseFast`. `dist/pii.wasm` is ~25 KB, inlined into the JS bundle.
+Hand-written kernels in `src/zig/wasm_exports.zig`, compiled with `-target wasm32-freestanding -mcpu=generic+simd128+relaxed_simd -O ReleaseFast`. `dist/textsift.wasm` is ~25 KB, inlined into the JS bundle.
 
 Kernels: embed lookup, RMSNorm, int4 matmul (three X×Out type variants), RoPE, banded attention with sinks, swiGLU with clamp, QMoE dispatch, scatter-add, cast, softmax, topK.

@@ -3,7 +3,7 @@ title: Backends
 description: Three execution engines behind the same public API.
 ---
 
-`pii-wasm` ships three interchangeable backends. The public API (`PrivacyFilter.create`, `redact`, `detect`) is identical across all three — the backend is an implementation detail picked at construction.
+`textsift` ships three interchangeable backends. The public API (`PrivacyFilter.create`, `redact`, `detect`) is identical across all three — the backend is an implementation detail picked at construction.
 
 | Backend | Device | Compute | When to use |
 |---|---|---|---|

@@ -7,7 +7,7 @@ All numbers measured on an M-series MacBook, Chromium 147 with `--enable-unsafe-
 
 ## Per-forward latency
 
-| Input length | tjs (WebGPU) | **pii-wasm (WebGPU)** | speedup | pii-wasm (WASM) |
+| Input length | tjs (WebGPU) | **textsift (WebGPU)** | speedup | textsift (WASM) |
 |---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
 | Short (~12 tokens) | 29 ms | **7 ms** | **4.1×** | 66 ms |
 | Medium (~25 tokens) | 39 ms | **12 ms** | **3.2×** | 158 ms |
@@ -20,12 +20,12 @@ Linear scaling with T — dispatch overhead is negligible at this model size, th
 | | First visit (HF CDN download) | Second visit (cache warm) |
 |---|---:|---:|
 | tjs | 13.3 s | 11.2 s |
-| pii-wasm (WASM) | 14.1 s | 1.8 s |
-| **pii-wasm (WebGPU)** | 14.1 s ¹ | **1.1 s** |
+| textsift (WASM) | 14.1 s | 1.8 s |
+| **textsift (WebGPU)** | 14.1 s ¹ | **1.1 s** |
 
 ¹ First visit is download-bound (~770 MB from HuggingFace CDN). All backends take similar time on the initial download; the difference is on repeat visits.
 
-**Second-visit cold start — pii-wasm webgpu is 10× faster than transformers.js.** The win comes from OPFS-backed caching: Cache API can't hold the 770 MB `.onnx_data` blob (quota rejects it), so we persist via OPFS where Chromium allocates ~60% of free disk per origin and Safari gives 1 GB.
+**Second-visit cold start — textsift webgpu is 10× faster than transformers.js.** The win comes from OPFS-backed caching: Cache API can't hold the 770 MB `.onnx_data` blob (quota rejects it), so we persist via OPFS where Chromium allocates ~60% of free disk per origin and Safari gives 1 GB.
 
 ## Warmup breakdown (cache hit)
 
@@ -42,7 +42,7 @@ From `tests/browser/webgpu-coldstart.spec.ts`, second warmup:
 
 ## Accuracy
 
-On the model's own benchmark (PII-Masking-300k), `openai/privacy-filter` reports **F1 96%** (state-of-the-art). All three pii-wasm backends produce byte-identical span output (`tests/browser/smoke.spec.ts`, `webgpu-smoke.spec.ts`), so the downstream accuracy is the model's.
+On the model's own benchmark (PII-Masking-300k), `openai/privacy-filter` reports **F1 96%** (state-of-the-art). All three textsift backends produce byte-identical span output (`tests/browser/smoke.spec.ts`, `webgpu-smoke.spec.ts`), so the downstream accuracy is the model's.
 
 Our forward-parity test (`tests/browser/webgpu-forward.spec.ts`) confirms argmax agrees on 32/32 tokens of deterministic input, with a logit RMS drift of 0.18 vs the WASM reference — the model's fp16 rounding noise compounding across 8 layers. Argmax (and thus Viterbi + span output) is preserved.

@@ -1,13 +1,13 @@
 ---
 title: Caveats
-description: What pii-wasm does well, and what it doesn't.
+description: What textsift does well, and what it doesn't.
 ---
 
 ## From OpenAI's own model card
 
 > Like all models, Privacy Filter can make mistakes. It may miss uncommon identifiers or ambiguous references, and it can over- or under-redact information when context is limited, especially in shorter text. In high-sensitivity areas such as legal, medical, and financial workflows, human review and domain-specific evaluation and fine-tuning remain important.
 
-`pii-wasm` is a production-grade inference engine for a model that itself is an aid, not an anonymization guarantee. Treat it as such.
+`textsift` is a production-grade inference engine for a model that itself is an aid, not an anonymization guarantee. Treat it as such.
 
 ## Known model-level gaps
 
@@ -29,7 +29,7 @@ description: What pii-wasm does well, and what it doesn't.
 - **Regex-level guarantees.** For known patterns (credit-card Luhn checks, US SSN structure validation), a regex library like `redact-pii` is faster and more deterministic. Run both for belt-and-braces.
 - **Multi-language deployments.** If your users write in Chinese, Arabic, or Hindi, consider Microsoft Presidio or a cloud DLP with native multilingual training.
 
-## When to use `pii-wasm`
+## When to use `textsift`
 
 - Client-side redaction in browser UIs (form fields, comment boxes, chat prompts before hitting a cloud LLM).
 - Browser-extension "warn before paste" prompts.

 OpenAI released [openai/privacy-filter](https://huggingface.co/openai/privacy-filter) on 2026-04-20 — a 1.5B-parameter MoE token classifier (50M active) for PII detection, Apache 2.0. It reaches **96-97% F1 on PII-Masking-300k** and runs on a laptop.
 
-The gap: the official SDK is Python. transformers.js runs it on WebGPU but has no CPU fallback. **`pii-wasm` is the only package that runs this model end-to-end in the browser with both WebGPU and CPU paths.**
+The gap: the official SDK is Python. transformers.js runs it on WebGPU but has no CPU fallback. **`textsift` is the only package that runs this model end-to-end in the browser with both WebGPU and CPU paths.**
 
 ## Features
 
@@ -52,7 +52,7 @@ The gap: the official SDK is Python. transformers.js runs it on WebGPU but has n
 ## 30-second example
 
 ```ts
-import { PrivacyFilter } from "pii-wasm";
+import { PrivacyFilter } from "textsift";
 
 const filter = await PrivacyFilter.create();
 const result = await filter.redact(
@@ -71,7 +71,7 @@ console.log(result.spans);
 
 ## How it compares
 
-| | tjs (WebGPU) | **pii-wasm (WebGPU)** | pii-wasm (WASM) |
+| | tjs (WebGPU) | **textsift (WebGPU)** | textsift (WASM) |
 |---|---:|---:|---:|
 | Forward — short | 29 ms | **7 ms** | 66 ms |
 | Forward — medium | 39 ms | **12 ms** | 158 ms |
@@ -80,4 +80,4 @@ console.log(result.spans);
 
 *M-series MacBook, Chromium 147, median of 5 runs after 2 warmups.*
 
-For **CPU-only environments** (corporate Chrome without WebGPU, Firefox with `dom.webgpu.enabled=false`, server-side Node), pii-wasm is the only working path — transformers.js fails session creation on this model's WASM device due to missing ORT-Web kernels (`GatherBlockQuantized`, `MatMulNBits`).
+For **CPU-only environments** (corporate Chrome without WebGPU, Firefox with `dom.webgpu.enabled=false`, server-side Node), textsift is the only working path — transformers.js fails session creation on this model's WASM device due to missing ORT-Web kernels (`GatherBlockQuantized`, `MatMulNBits`).

@@ -6,14 +6,14 @@ description: Install, detect PII, redact text — in three code blocks.
 ## Install
 
 ```sh
-npm install pii-wasm
+npm install textsift
 # or: pnpm add / bun add / yarn add
 ```
 
 ## Redact
 
 ```ts
-import { PrivacyFilter } from "pii-wasm";
+import { PrivacyFilter } from "textsift";
 
 const filter = await PrivacyFilter.create();
 
@@ -55,7 +55,7 @@ const gpu = await PrivacyFilter.create({ backend: "webgpu" });
 const wasm = await PrivacyFilter.create({ backend: "wasm" });
 ```
 
-See the [Backends](/pii-wasm/backends/) page for the full compatibility table.
+See the [Backends](/textsift/backends/) page for the full compatibility table.
 
 ## Dispose when done

@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 # Roadmap
 
-This is the execution plan for `@yourorg/pii-wasm`. Stage 0 ships a working
+This is the execution plan for `@teamchong/textsift`. Stage 0 ships a working
 library by wrapping transformers.js behind the public API. Stages 1–3
 replace internals with progressively faster and more compact inference
 paths. The public API doesn't change between stages.
@@ -41,7 +41,7 @@ inputs through the same output pipeline as upstream.
       labelled fixture are model behaviour, not a wiring defect.
 - [x] Demo page (`demo/index.html`) — browser-tested via CDP.
 
-**Ship gate (met).** Parity confirmed transitively: pii-wasm ↔
+**Ship gate (met).** Parity confirmed transitively: textsift ↔
 transformers.js pipeline is byte-exact on the same logits; the
 pipeline shares the model with `opf` CLI so decoding agreement is
 mechanical. Chrome benchmark captured in `docs/benchmarks.md`
@@ -80,9 +80,9 @@ Target: 1.5–2× faster than Stage 0 Chrome warm-inference
 - [x] `src/zig/wasm_exports.zig` — bump allocator (`heap_init` /
       `alloc` / `reset` / `heap_used`), plumbing smoke-test helpers
       (`echo`, `sum_i32`).
-- [x] Built via `npm run build:zig` → `dist/pii.wasm`
+- [x] Built via `npm run build:zig` → `dist/textsift.wasm`
       (`wasm32-freestanding`, `simd128` + `relaxed_simd`).
-- [x] JS bridge `src/js/backends/wasm.ts` with `loadPiiWasm()`
+- [x] JS bridge `src/js/backends/wasm.ts` with `loadTextsift()`
       loader + `WasmBackend` class (`forward()` throws until
       kernels land).
 - [x] End-to-end round-trip verified: echo ABI, JS→WASM memory
@@ -213,7 +213,7 @@ baselines and blocks on regression. Zig unit tests for pure helpers
       `bench.spec.ts`, `public-api.html` + spec) running under
       Chromium with `--enable-unsafe-webgpu --use-angle=metal` so
       the transformers.js WebGPU path is real, not swiftshader.
-- [x] Inline `dist/pii.wasm` as a Uint8Array via
+- [x] Inline `dist/textsift.wasm` as a Uint8Array via
       `scripts/inline-wasm.mjs` — the JS bundle ships the .wasm bytes
       so there's no separate HTTP round-trip and no URL-resolution
       quirk when the library is re-bundled.
@@ -260,7 +260,7 @@ Replace the custom `pii-weights.bin` path with direct reading of
       exports (`weights_load` et al.) + `readU32LE`/`readU64LE`
       helpers removed. Composition fixture specs pruned from
       `tests/fixtures/manifest.json` (per-kernel fixtures stay).
-      `WeightDType.I4_BLOCK32_SYM` removed. dist/pii.wasm: 25 KB.
+      `WeightDType.I4_BLOCK32_SYM` removed. dist/textsift.wasm: 25 KB.
 
 **OPFS note.** With Stage 3 NO-GO (2026-04-23), the weight size
 Stage 1 carries equals what Stage 0 already ships: ~772 MB

+    "build:zig-mt": "mise exec -- zig build-exe -target wasm32-freestanding -mcpu=generic+simd128+relaxed_simd+atomics+bulk_memory -O ReleaseFast -fstrip -flto src/zig/wasm_exports.zig -cflags -O3 -mrelaxed-simd -flto -- src/c/fma.c --shared-memory --import-memory --initial-memory=4194304 --max-memory=2147483648 --export=__stack_pointer --name textsift-mt -fno-entry -rdynamic && mv textsift-mt.wasm dist/",

+export const TEXTSIFT_BYTES: Uint8Array = new Uint8Array([
